paragraphTranslations.zh: 他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
paragraphTranslations.en: He said he had met a similar problem too.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这篇短文讲的是《在中国寄快递》。
sentenceExplanations.en: This reading is about 'Sending a Package in China'.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This reading is about 'Sending a Package in China'.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 上个周末，我因为一件小事去了南京。
sentenceExplanations.en: Last weekend, I went to Nanjing because of a small matter.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Last weekend, I went to Nanjing because of a small matter.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
sentenceExplanations.en: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
sentenceExplanations.en: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
grammar.exampleEn: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
sentenceExplanations.en: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
sentenceExplanations.en: I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
sentenceExplanations.en: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

grammar.label: Sufficient condition
grammar.explanationEn: The opening phrase means “as long as.” Together with the result marker, it states that one condition is enough for the result to follow.
grammar.exampleZh: 地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
grammar.exampleEn: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
sentenceExplanations.en: But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 后来我问了一位司机。
sentenceExplanations.en: Later I asked a chauffeur for help.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Later I asked a chauffeur for help.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 司机很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
sentenceExplanations.en: The chauffeur was very kind and told me the way directly.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The chauffeur was very kind and told me the way directly.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 司机很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
grammar.exampleEn: The chauffeur was very kind and told me the way directly.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
sentenceExplanations.en: It turned out I had taken the wrong turn.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It turned out I had taken the wrong turn.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这让我有点儿不好意思。
sentenceExplanations.en: That made me a little embarrassed.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: That made me a little embarrassed.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
sentenceExplanations.en: But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
sentenceExplanations.en: After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我的司机还没有来。
sentenceExplanations.en: My chauffeur had not arrived yet.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: My chauffeur had not arrived yet.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
sentenceExplanations.en: I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
sentenceExplanations.en: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.

grammar.label: Time frame
grammar.explanationEn: This time phrase follows an action, event, or time expression and sets the time frame for the main clause: “when …”.
grammar.exampleZh: 等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
grammar.exampleEn: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
sentenceExplanations.en: The shop had hot tea and simple bread.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The shop had hot tea and simple bread.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
sentenceExplanations.en: I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
sentenceExplanations.en: At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 司机来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
sentenceExplanations.en: After the chauffeur arrived, we finished the matter together.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After the chauffeur arrived, we finished the matter together.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 事情本身只用了半个小时。
sentenceExplanations.en: The task itself took only half an hour.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The task itself took only half an hour.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
sentenceExplanations.en: But for that half hour, I learned a lot.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But for that half hour, I learned a lot.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我学会了提前看路线。
sentenceExplanations.en: I learned to check the route in advance.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I learned to check the route in advance.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
sentenceExplanations.en: I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
sentenceExplanations.en: Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 下午我们没有马上回去。
sentenceExplanations.en: In the afternoon, we did not go back right away.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: In the afternoon, we did not go back right away.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们在南京的一条老街走了走。
sentenceExplanations.en: We walked around an old street in Nanjing.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We walked around an old street in Nanjing.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
sentenceExplanations.en: There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
sentenceExplanations.en: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
sentenceExplanations.en: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
sentenceExplanations.en: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
sentenceExplanations.en: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
grammar.exampleEn: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
sentenceExplanations.en: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

grammar.label: Degree complement
grammar.explanationEn: The complement marker introduces a phrase that describes the degree or manner of an action. It answers how well, how quickly, or to what extent something is done.
grammar.exampleZh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
grammar.exampleEn: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
sentenceExplanations.en: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
sentenceExplanations.en: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

grammar.label: Negative instruction
grammar.explanationEn: This form placed before a verb tells someone not to do that action. It is the standard negative form for an instruction or request.
grammar.exampleZh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
grammar.exampleEn: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
sentenceExplanations.en: Because many troubles later become stories.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Because many troubles later become stories.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
sentenceExplanations.en: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
grammar.exampleEn: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这并不说明自己能力差。
sentenceExplanations.en: This does not mean one's ability is poor.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This does not mean one's ability is poor.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
sentenceExplanations.en: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
sentenceExplanations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
sentenceExplanations.en: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
sentenceExplanations.en: This method is simple but useful.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This method is simple but useful.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
sentenceExplanations.en: It gives me more security in new places.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It gives me more security in new places.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
sentenceExplanations.en: I am also more willing to travel alone.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I am also more willing to travel alone.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
sentenceExplanations.en: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
sentenceExplanations.en: Later, I told another friend about this experience.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
grammar.exampleEn: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
sentenceExplanations.en: He said he had met a similar problem too.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: He said he had met a similar problem too.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the main topic of this reading?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 在中国寄快递
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: It mainly discusses sending a package in china.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The title and repeated details point to the central topic.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the writer's final thought?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The final sentence usually gives the conclusion or reflection.

summaryEn: An HSK 3 reading titled “Why People Like High-Speed Rail”.
textZh:
这篇短文讲的是《为什么大家喜欢高铁》。
上个周末，我因为一件小事去了广州。
出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
后来我问了一位店员。
店员很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
这让我有点儿不好意思。
不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
我的店员还没有来。
我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
店员来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
事情本身只用了半个小时。
可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
我学会了提前看路线。
我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
下午我们没有马上回去。
我们在广州的一条老街走了走。
街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
这并不说明自己能力差。
相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
translation: This reading is about 'Why People Like High-Speed Rail'. Last weekend, I went to Guangzhou because of a small matter. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a shop assistant for help. The shop assistant was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My shop assistant had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the shop assistant arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We walked around an old street in Guangzhou. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.
translationEn: This reading is about 'Why People Like High-Speed Rail'. Last weekend, I went to Guangzhou because of a small matter. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a shop assistant for help. The shop assistant was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My shop assistant had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the shop assistant arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We walked around an old street in Guangzhou. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
paragraphTranslations.en: I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
paragraphTranslations.en: I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
paragraphTranslations.en: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
paragraphTranslations.en: The shop had hot tea and simple bread.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
paragraphTranslations.en: I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
paragraphTranslations.en: At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 店员来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
paragraphTranslations.en: After the shop assistant arrived, we finished the matter together.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 事情本身只用了半个小时。
paragraphTranslations.en: The task itself took only half an hour.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
paragraphTranslations.en: But for that half hour, I learned a lot.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我学会了提前看路线。
paragraphTranslations.en: I learned to check the route in advance.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
paragraphTranslations.en: I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
paragraphTranslations.en: Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 下午我们没有马上回去。
paragraphTranslations.en: In the afternoon, we did not go back right away.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们在广州的一条老街走了走。
paragraphTranslations.en: We walked around an old street in Guangzhou.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
paragraphTranslations.en: There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
paragraphTranslations.en: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
paragraphTranslations.en: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
paragraphTranslations.en: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
paragraphTranslations.en: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
paragraphTranslations.en: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
paragraphTranslations.en: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
paragraphTranslations.en: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
paragraphTranslations.en: Because many troubles later become stories.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
paragraphTranslations.en: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
paragraphTranslations.en: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这并不说明自己能力差。
paragraphTranslations.en: This does not mean one's ability is poor.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
paragraphTranslations.en: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
paragraphTranslations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
paragraphTranslations.en: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
paragraphTranslations.en: This method is simple but useful.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
paragraphTranslations.en: It gives me more security in new places.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
paragraphTranslations.en: I am also more willing to travel alone.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
paragraphTranslations.en: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
paragraphTranslations.en: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
paragraphTranslations.en: He said he had met a similar problem too.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
paragraphTranslations.en: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这篇短文讲的是《为什么大家喜欢高铁》。
sentenceExplanations.en: This reading is about 'Why People Like High-Speed Rail'.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This reading is about 'Why People Like High-Speed Rail'.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 上个周末，我因为一件小事去了广州。
sentenceExplanations.en: Last weekend, I went to Guangzhou because of a small matter.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Last weekend, I went to Guangzhou because of a small matter.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
sentenceExplanations.en: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
sentenceExplanations.en: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
grammar.exampleEn: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
sentenceExplanations.en: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

grammar.label: Sufficient condition
grammar.explanationEn: The opening phrase means “as long as.” Together with the result marker, it states that one condition is enough for the result to follow.

grammar.exampleEn: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The shop assistant was very kind and told me the way directly.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这让我有点儿不好意思。

summaryEn: An HSK 3 reading titled “Choices in the Dining Hall”.
textZh:
这篇短文讲的是《食堂里的选择》。
上个周末，我因为一件小事去了北京。
出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
后来我问了一位室友。
室友很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
这让我有点儿不好意思。
不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
我的室友还没有来。
我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
室友来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
事情本身只用了半个小时。
可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
我学会了提前看路线。
我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
下午我们没有马上回去。
我们在北京的一条老街走了走。
街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
这并不说明自己能力差。
相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
translation: This reading is about 'Choices in the Dining Hall'. Last weekend, I went to Beijing for a small errand. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a roommate for help. The roommate was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My roommate had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the roommate arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We strolled along an old street in Beijing. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a roommate for help. The roommate was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My roommate had not arrived yet. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这篇短文讲的是《食堂里的选择》。
paragraphTranslations.en: This reading is about 'Choices in the Dining Hall'.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 上个周末，我因为一件小事去了北京。
paragraphTranslations.en: Last weekend, I went to Beijing for a small errand.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
paragraphTranslations.en: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
paragraphTranslations.en: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
paragraphTranslations.en: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
paragraphTranslations.en: I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
paragraphTranslations.en: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
paragraphTranslations.en: But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 后来我问了一位室友。
paragraphTranslations.en: Later I asked a roommate for help.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 室友很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
paragraphTranslations.en: The roommate was very kind and told me the way directly.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
paragraphTranslations.en: It turned out I had taken the wrong turn.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这让我有点儿不好意思。
paragraphTranslations.en: That made me a little embarrassed.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
paragraphTranslations.en: But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
paragraphTranslations.en: After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我的室友还没有来。
paragraphTranslations.en: My roommate had not arrived yet.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
paragraphTranslations.en: I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
paragraphTranslations.en: I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
paragraphTranslations.en: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
paragraphTranslations.en: The shop had hot tea and simple bread.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
paragraphTranslations.en: I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
paragraphTranslations.en: At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 室友来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
paragraphTranslations.en: After the roommate arrived, we finished the matter together.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 事情本身只用了半个小时。
paragraphTranslations.en: The task itself took only half an hour.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
paragraphTranslations.en: But for that half hour, I learned a lot.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我学会了提前看路线。
paragraphTranslations.en: I learned to check the route in advance.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
paragraphTranslations.en: I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
paragraphTranslations.en: Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 下午我们没有马上回去。
paragraphTranslations.en: In the afternoon, we did not go back right away.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们在北京的一条老街走了走。
paragraphTranslations.en: We strolled along an old street in Beijing.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
paragraphTranslations.en: There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
paragraphTranslations.en: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
paragraphTranslations.en: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
paragraphTranslations.en: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
paragraphTranslations.en: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
paragraphTranslations.en: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
paragraphTranslations.en: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
paragraphTranslations.en: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
paragraphTranslations.en: Because many troubles later become stories.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
paragraphTranslations.en: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这并不说明自己能力差。
paragraphTranslations.en: This does not mean one's ability is poor.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
paragraphTranslations.en: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
paragraphTranslations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
paragraphTranslations.en: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
paragraphTranslations.en: This method is simple but useful.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
paragraphTranslations.en: It gives me more security in new places.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
paragraphTranslations.en: I am also more willing to travel alone.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
paragraphTranslations.en: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
paragraphTranslations.en: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
paragraphTranslations.en: He said he had met a similar problem too.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
paragraphTranslations.en: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
paragraphTranslations.en: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
grammar.exampleEn: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the main topic of this reading?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 食堂里的选择
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: It mainly discusses choices in the dining hall.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The title and repeated details point to the central topic.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the writer's final thought?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 这并不说明自己能力差。
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: This does not mean one's ability is poor.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The final sentence usually gives the conclusion or reflection.

summaryEn: An HSK 3 reading titled “My Chinese Improved”.
textZh:
这篇短文讲的是《我的中文进步了》。
上个周末，我因为一件小事去了上海。
出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
后来我问了一位同学。
同学很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
这让我有点儿不好意思。
不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
我的同学还没有来。
我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
同学来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
事情本身只用了半个小时。
可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
我学会了提前看路线。
我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
下午我们没有马上回去。
我们在上海的一条老街走了走。
街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
这并不说明自己能力差。
相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
translation: This reading is about 'My Chinese Improved'. Last weekend, I went to Shanghai for a small errand. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a classmate for help. The classmate was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My classmate had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the classmate arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We strolled along an old street in Shanghai. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

paragraphTranslations.zh: 那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。

paragraphTranslations.zh: 我学会了提前看路线。

paragraphTranslations.zh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
grammar.exampleEn: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。

grammar.label: Sufficient condition
grammar.explanationEn: The opening phrase means “as long as.” Together with the result marker, it states that one condition is enough for the result to follow.
grammar.exampleZh: 地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
grammar.exampleEn: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The classmate was very kind and told me the way directly.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 同学很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
grammar.exampleEn: The classmate was very kind and told me the way directly.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
sentenceExplanations.en: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
sentenceExplanations.en: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
sentenceExplanations.en: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
sentenceExplanations.en: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
grammar.exampleEn: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
sentenceExplanations.en: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

grammar.label: Degree complement
grammar.explanationEn: The complement marker introduces a phrase that describes the degree or manner of an action. It answers how well, how quickly, or to what extent something is done.
grammar.exampleZh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
grammar.exampleEn: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
sentenceExplanations.en: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
sentenceExplanations.en: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

grammar.label: Negative instruction
grammar.explanationEn: This form placed before a verb tells someone not to do that action. It is the standard negative form for an instruction or request.
grammar.exampleZh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
grammar.exampleEn: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
sentenceExplanations.en: Because many troubles later become stories.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Because many troubles later become stories.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这并不说明自己能力差。
sentenceExplanations.en: This does not mean one's ability is poor.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This does not mean one's ability is poor.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
sentenceExplanations.en: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
sentenceExplanations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
sentenceExplanations.en: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
sentenceExplanations.en: This method is simple but useful.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This method is simple but useful.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
sentenceExplanations.en: It gives me more security in new places.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It gives me more security in new places.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
sentenceExplanations.en: I am also more willing to travel alone.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I am also more willing to travel alone.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
sentenceExplanations.en: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
sentenceExplanations.en: Later, I told another friend about this experience.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
grammar.exampleEn: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
sentenceExplanations.en: He said he had met a similar problem too.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: He said he had met a similar problem too.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
sentenceExplanations.en: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
grammar.exampleEn: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
sentenceExplanations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the main topic of this reading?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 我的中文进步了
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: It mainly discusses my chinese improved.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The title and repeated details point to the central topic.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the writer's final thought?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The final sentence usually gives the conclusion or reflection.

summaryEn: An HSK 3 reading titled “Registering at a Hospital”.
textZh:
这篇短文讲的是《去医院挂号》。
上个周末，我因为一件小事去了杭州。
出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
后来我问了一位朋友。
朋友很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
这让我有点儿不好意思。
不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
我的朋友还没有来。
我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
朋友来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
事情本身只用了半个小时。
可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
我学会了提前看路线。
我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
下午我们没有马上回去。
我们在杭州的一条老街走了走。
街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
这并不说明自己能力差。
从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
translation: This reading is about 'Registering at a Hospital'. Last weekend, I went to Hangzhou because of a small matter. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a friend for help. The friend was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My friend had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the friend arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We walked around an old street in Hangzhou. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

The friend was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My friend had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the friend arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这篇短文讲的是《去医院挂号》。
sentenceExplanations.en: This reading is about 'Registering at a Hospital'.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This reading is about 'Registering at a Hospital'.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 上个周末，我因为一件小事去了杭州。
sentenceExplanations.en: Last weekend, I went to Hangzhou because of a small matter.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Last weekend, I went to Hangzhou because of a small matter.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
sentenceExplanations.en: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
sentenceExplanations.en: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

grammar.label: Only then / not until
grammar.explanationEn: This word emphasizes that something happens only then, later than expected, or after a necessary condition has been met.
grammar.exampleZh: 可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
grammar.exampleEn: But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
sentenceExplanations.en: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The first problem was that I could not find the right place.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
sentenceExplanations.en: I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
sentenceExplanations.en: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

grammar.label: Sufficient condition
grammar.explanationEn: The opening phrase means “as long as.” Together with the result marker, it states that one condition is enough for the result to follow.
grammar.exampleZh: 地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
grammar.exampleEn: The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
sentenceExplanations.en: But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 后来我问了一位朋友。
sentenceExplanations.en: Later I asked a friend for help.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Later I asked a friend for help.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 朋友很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
sentenceExplanations.en: The friend was very kind and told me the way directly.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The friend was very kind and told me the way directly.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 朋友很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
grammar.exampleEn: The friend was very kind and told me the way directly.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
sentenceExplanations.en: It turned out I had taken the wrong turn.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It turned out I had taken the wrong turn.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这让我有点儿不好意思。
sentenceExplanations.en: That made me a little embarrassed.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: That made me a little embarrassed.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
sentenceExplanations.en: But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
sentenceExplanations.en: After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我的朋友还没有来。
sentenceExplanations.en: My friend had not arrived yet.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: My friend had not arrived yet.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
sentenceExplanations.en: I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
sentenceExplanations.en: I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
sentenceExplanations.en: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.

grammar.label: Time frame
grammar.explanationEn: This time phrase follows an action, event, or time expression and sets the time frame for the main clause: “when …”.
grammar.exampleZh: 等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
grammar.exampleEn: While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
sentenceExplanations.en: The shop had hot tea and simple bread.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The shop had hot tea and simple bread.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
sentenceExplanations.en: I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
sentenceExplanations.en: At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 朋友来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
sentenceExplanations.en: After the friend arrived, we finished the matter together.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After the friend arrived, we finished the matter together.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 事情本身只用了半个小时。
sentenceExplanations.en: The task itself took only half an hour.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: The task itself took only half an hour.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
sentenceExplanations.en: But for that half hour, I learned a lot.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: But for that half hour, I learned a lot.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我学会了提前看路线。
sentenceExplanations.en: I learned to check the route in advance.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I learned to check the route in advance.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
sentenceExplanations.en: I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
sentenceExplanations.en: Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 下午我们没有马上回去。
sentenceExplanations.en: In the afternoon, we did not go back right away.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: In the afternoon, we did not go back right away.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们在杭州的一条老街走了走。
sentenceExplanations.en: We walked around an old street in Hangzhou.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We walked around an old street in Hangzhou.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
sentenceExplanations.en: There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
sentenceExplanations.en: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
sentenceExplanations.en: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: My friend said this is what makes travel interesting.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
sentenceExplanations.en: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
sentenceExplanations.en: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
grammar.exampleEn: When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
sentenceExplanations.en: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

grammar.label: Degree complement
grammar.explanationEn: The complement marker introduces a phrase that describes the degree or manner of an action. It answers how well, how quickly, or to what extent something is done.
grammar.exampleZh: 我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
grammar.exampleEn: I did not write much, but I wrote carefully.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
sentenceExplanations.en: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
sentenceExplanations.en: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

grammar.label: Negative instruction
grammar.explanationEn: This form placed before a verb tells someone not to do that action. It is the standard negative form for an instruction or request.
grammar.exampleZh: 同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
grammar.exampleEn: At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
sentenceExplanations.en: Because many troubles later become stories.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Because many troubles later become stories.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
sentenceExplanations.en: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
sentenceExplanations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
sentenceExplanations.en: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
sentenceExplanations.en: This method is simple but useful.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This method is simple but useful.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
sentenceExplanations.en: It gives me more security in new places.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: It gives me more security in new places.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
sentenceExplanations.en: I am also more willing to travel alone.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: I am also more willing to travel alone.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
sentenceExplanations.en: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Sometimes growth starts from small things like these.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
sentenceExplanations.en: Later, I told another friend about this experience.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

grammar.label: Disposing of an object
grammar.explanationEn: This construction brings a specific object before the verb to emphasize what is done with it and the resulting action or state.
grammar.exampleZh: 后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
grammar.exampleEn: Later, I told another friend about this experience.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
sentenceExplanations.en: He said he had met a similar problem too.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: He said he had met a similar problem too.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
sentenceExplanations.en: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

grammar.label: Condition and result
grammar.explanationEn: The first connector introduces a condition; the second marks what happens when that condition is met.
grammar.exampleZh: 如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
grammar.exampleEn: If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
sentenceExplanations.en: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 这并不说明自己能力差。
sentenceExplanations.en: This does not mean one's ability is poor.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: This does not mean one's ability is poor.

sentenceExplanations.zh: 从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
sentenceExplanations.en: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
sentenceExplanations.naturalMeaning: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the main topic of this reading?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 去医院挂号
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: It mainly discusses registering at a hospital.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The title and repeated details point to the central topic.

comprehensionQuestions.questionEn: What is the writer's final thought?
comprehensionQuestions.answer: 从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
comprehensionQuestions.answerEn: After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
comprehensionQuestions.explanationEn: The final sentence usually gives the conclusion or reflection.

summaryEn: An HSK 3 reading titled “A Short Weekend Trip”.
textZh:
这篇短文讲的是《周末的短途旅行》。
上个周末，我因为一件小事去了成都。
出发以前，我以为事情会很简单。
可是到了以后，我才发现计划没有那么容易。
第一件麻烦事是我找不到正确的地方。
我打开手机地图，看了好几遍路线。
地图上说只要走十分钟就到。
可是我走了二十分钟，还是没有看到门口。
后来我问了一位老师。
老师很热心，直接把路告诉了我。
原来我刚才走错了一个路口。
这让我有点儿不好意思。
不过我也明白，在不熟悉的城市里问人并不丢脸。
到了地方以后，我先在门口等了十分钟。
我的老师还没有来。
我给他发消息，他说地铁晚了一点。
我没有生气，因为旅行中常常会有小变化。
等人的时候，我看见旁边有一家小店。
店里有热茶，也有简单的面包。
我买了一杯茶，坐下来休息。
那一刻，我突然觉得慢一点也不错。
老师来了以后，我们一起办完了事情。
事情本身只用了半个小时。
可是为了这半个小时，我学到了很多。
我学会了提前看路线。
我也学会了遇到问题时先冷静。
最重要的是，我发现陌生人有时候会给你很大的帮助。
下午我们没有马上回去。
我们在成都的一条老街走了走。
街上有卖小吃的人，也有拍照的游客。
我买了一份小吃，味道比我想的好。
朋友说，这就是旅行有意思的地方。
你原来只想完成一件事，最后却记住了很多小细节。
晚上回到家，我把今天的经历写在本子上。
我写得不长，但是写得很认真。
以后再去新的地方，我会准备得更好。
同时，我也希望自己不要怕麻烦。
因为很多麻烦，后来都会变成故事。
从那以后，我出门以前会做一个小清单。
清单上有地址、时间、交通方法和重要电话。
这个方法很简单，但是很有用。
它让我在新的地方更有安全感。
我也更敢一个人去旅行了。
有时候，成长就是从这些小事开始的。
后来，我把这次经历告诉了另一个朋友。
他说，他也遇到过差不多的问题。
我们发现，很多麻烦不是因为事情太大，而是因为准备太少。
如果出发前多看一遍信息，可能就能少走很多路。
如果遇到问题以后马上问人，也能节省时间。
这并不说明自己能力差。
相反，这说明你愿意解决问题。
从那以后，我们出门以前会做一个小清单。
translation: This reading is about 'A Short Weekend Trip'. Last weekend, I went to Chengdu because of a small matter. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a teacher for help. The teacher was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My teacher had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the teacher arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We walked around an old street in Chengdu. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
translationEn: This reading is about 'A Short Weekend Trip'. Last weekend, I went to Chengdu because of a small matter. Before leaving, I thought everything would be simple. But after I arrived, I realized the plan was not that easy. The first problem was that I could not find the right place. I opened the map on my phone and looked at the route several times. The map said it would take only ten minutes to walk there. But I walked for twenty minutes and still did not see the entrance. Later I asked a teacher for help. The teacher was very kind and told me the way directly. It turned out I had taken the wrong turn. That made me a little embarrassed. But I also understood that asking people in an unfamiliar city is not shameful. After arriving, I waited at the door for ten minutes. My teacher had not arrived yet. I messaged him, and he said the subway was a little late. I did not get angry, because small changes often happen during travel. While waiting, I saw a small shop nearby. The shop had hot tea and simple bread. I bought a cup of tea and sat down to rest. At that moment, I suddenly felt that going a little slower was not bad. After the teacher arrived, we finished the matter together. The task itself took only half an hour. But for that half hour, I learned a lot. I learned to check the route in advance. I also learned to stay calm when I meet a problem. Most importantly, I found that strangers can sometimes help you a lot. In the afternoon, we did not go back right away. We walked around an old street in Chengdu. There were people selling snacks and tourists taking photos. I bought a snack, and it tasted better than I expected. My friend said this is what makes travel interesting. At first you only want to finish one thing, but in the end you remember many small details. When I got home that evening, I wrote the experience in my notebook. I did not write much, but I wrote carefully. Next time I go somewhere new, I will prepare better. At the same time, I hope I will not be afraid of trouble. Because many troubles later become stories. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist. On the list are the address, time, transportation method, and important phone numbers. This method is simple but useful. It gives me more security in new places. I am also more willing to travel alone. Sometimes growth starts from small things like these. Later, I told another friend about this experience. He said he had met a similar problem too. We realized many troubles happen not because the matter is too big, but because preparation is too little. If you check the information one more time before leaving, you may walk much less. If you ask someone right after meeting a problem, you can also save time. This does not mean one's ability is poor. On the contrary, it shows you are willing to solve problems. After that, before going out, I make a small checklist.
